summary: A legendary NYE ’76 show – funk‑filled Playin’ In The Band, a monster Good Lovin' > Samson transition and soaring Help/Slip/NFA/Dew – captured on an audience FM tape with solid, if not pristine, sound.
review: Fans rave about this New Year’s Eve performance as one of the best ’76 concerts, highlighting the explosive Playin’ In The Band jam, the unforgettable Good Lovin' > Samson segue and the extended Help‑Slip‑Not Fade Away‑Dew sequence.  The recording is an FM broadcast (audience source) that delivers decent clarity; a few reviewers note hiss, PCM‑generation artifacts and a bass‑heavy mix, but overall it remains very listenable.  Energy builds from a slow start into sustained funk and improvisational fire throughout both sets.

ai_rating.rationale: Over 20 reviews overwhelmingly praise the musical performance (show rating ~4.8) while noting a good‑to‑fair recording quality (~4.0). Weighted average yields ~4.6 stars with consistent sentiment across reviewers.

recording_quality.technical_notes: FM broadcast capture; some hiss and PCM‑generation artifacts mentioned, bass can dominate the mix, but overall clarity is respectable and the tape sounds ‘decent’ to ‘excellent’ to most listeners.

standout_songs: Help > Slip > Not Fade Away > Dew
show_quality.setlist_flow: Both sets start a bit slow, then the band builds momentum—LL Rain fuels the first set climax, while the second set erupts with a massive Good Lovin'‑Samson groove and an extended Help/Slip/NFA/Dew jam that carries the show to its peak.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for soaring leads, funky riffs on the Good Lovin' transition, vocal highlights (especially on Promised Land) and driving the improvisational flow.
band_member_comments.Phil: Bass is front‑and‑center; many note a dominant mix that lets his thumping lines shine throughout the night.
band_member_comments.Bob: Solid rhythm work and vocal contributions; noted for a mini solo behind Jerry’s chord solo and strong presence on Samson and other jams.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith shows early timing quirks but recovers with higher‑note flourishes later; overall keyboards add color to the jams.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill Kreutzmann and Mickey Hart provide a cooking backbeat, two brief drum solos, though some reviewers feel pacing slips toward the end.
